All right everybody, let's get into the world of PPV traffic also known as CPV traffic, okay, and let me teach you how to dominate your space in the PPV world Now first let's get into kind of what is PPV many people haven't used it. It's kind of Kind of a fun thing that I don't think it's talked about very often But it's a great traffic source and I'm gonna teach you how to do it here by the time You're done with this course. You should be able just to grab an ad provider and start running with it. So The upside of PPV traffic is you can get really cheap clicks to your site, okay? I mean, we're talking like one cent five cents a click to your site and You know if you get it targeted right and we're gonna go through all that so it's potentially very inexpensive traffic highly targeted that you that you can get so What PPV traffic is in a nutshell is adware that's installed on a consenting users computer So for example somebody's trying to download some freebies like some games or some movies or things like that well the The PPV network, you know people who are collecting these users offer these free things, right? That's the value they provide say hey, here's this free game. Here's this free download Here's this free ringtone things like that and if you download on the new consent to to have that software installed on your computer Which is then going to serve to you? ads in exchange So from that from that consenting relationship you get a whole network of people In that these ad providers can then deliver ads to and so what you're doing is you're buying ad space from that So that's what PPV traffic is in a nutshell And where it comes from so they're not on paid ads it on page ads It's not like you're you're buying banner space on an ad Or on a page or anything like that instead you're You're buying pop-up ads or in text ads and I'll go into that and show you tell you what they're about But that's what makes us different from from like, you know banner banner buying or media buying things like that so The different pieces of PPV traffic is that you need to find an ad provider and get an account set up with them And there's a variety there's a bunch of different ones the most of them that that I've heard of or dealt with Have you set up an account so you pre-fund your account with a couple hundred bucks or a thousand bucks? I mean depends on the provider. I know there's one that let you ones that let you in with is as little as a hundred dollars and some that Require that you have at least a thousand dollars to posit it into your ad spend account before you can create an account so You have to look at the different providers see what their Requirements are and see which one fits your needs and budget and things like that All and then the next part is your ads and we'll take a look at a couple ads and show you the different types of ads So you can have an idea of how it could work for you The next thing is the target so this is going to be the key to PPV traffic is understanding your targets and and how to maximize your ROI in various targets that what you return on investment for this For this target and that target and we'll go into this I mean you could you could play the game where you want to be first and foremost on every target Which is expensive but get you high volume or you can take it one of a backseat and get inexpensive exposure at a little bit less volume so We're going to cover both of those and then lastly the optimization of making on that all come together So the ad providers available to you are many and here's just a List to get you started traffic events my favorite the one I'll be showing you some examples in direct CPV lead impact add on network media traffic Icewater media things like that. So I'm sure there's more. This is just a brief list To get you get you started get your research going Again, I've heard great things about traffic events. It's my favorite so That is that when it comes to ad providers now like I said again these ad providers are Are continually building their their networks of people who have Their software installed on their on their devices so that they can serve your ads to them so Always growing ever changing. It's a good idea to see if you can get some information on the demographic of Your ad providers network the audience you're going to be hitting or they are the younger or the older They male or the female is it just a blend to the ad provider notice any demographic Specifics that that could help you out understanding Understanding that in your traffic. So that's a good thing to look into but let's get into the ads now that you've had chance to take a look at the ad providers and in select one so There's two kinds of ads with PPV traffic The first I'm going to talk about is contextual pop-ups and the second is interstitial ads or in text ads Okay, so the contextual pop-ups are pop-ups that show up When The user of the software so when the users browsing around and They happen to search for a specific keyword or keyword string or even visit a specific URL Depending on how you've targeted your ad The Software will pop up in a new browser window Your your ad and display it to the user serve it to the user so You'll need to know from your ad provider what the size of that pop-up window is so you can Create your banner or landing page Or video or whatever you want to pop Not to fit so it fits nicely in the space. I know traffic bands is 800 600 by 800 so You know that's the space you have to work with there So it's a pretty large window of other ones have smaller windows other ones have larger windows So traffic bands is a 800 by 600 and And that's what you have to work with on that pop-up and Then the next is the interstitial ads or the in-text ads and these text ads The way they work is when there's a body of text. Let's say somebody's reading a Wikipedia article Or something like that on how to improve their golf swing, but when that phrase comes up that you're targeting You know improve golf swing or something like that Whatever that phrase is that you're targeting when that shows up in a body of text it can be bolded underlined highlighted and when they mouse over That text is your ad displays and a little like covering bubble little Lightbox is what it kind of looks like so it pops up and and grabs the attention of the viewer so they Hopefully click on it which then takes them to the destination page whether that be You know a landing page and I would recommend for your ads Test out a variety test out some large banner ads That will pop up the right size for your ad provider so a large banner that and I'll show you an example of Netflix banner You know that simple that's called action directed that's benefit-driven Pop-up display banner the other thing I would try is and you can do this with a Here's a website that you know giving credit scores So you can click on this, you know this big orange button They've got here check your credit score credit score and here's some important information things like that But what happens is that the the viewer here who's using this browser typed in this URL went to this page But they've got the Ad provider software and sub on their computer. So as soon as the phrase free credit score shows up The browser the software actually highlights it and underlines it And if ever they mouse over it this big ad pops up, right? So in essence you can sit directly on your competitors page if you know your competitors pages their URLs Some phrases that they're using on those pages. You can actually Use that as a target and target this URL and display your ad on their site So this is an in-text ad here because inside of this body of text a Phrase is highlighted or underlined and when moused over your ad shows up You're gonna want this to be very relevant to the page, you know to what the mouse over is So make sure that your ad is relevant to the target that you're using That you're bidding for As well as huge call to action here, you know if you your credit score graphical button-based very good Also test just a blue highlighted link inside of here I've heard rumors that that converts better again. You're gonna want to test on your audience Instead of a button is that would be to have a blue link That's obviously clickable people are used to that and I've heard it works well So this is an example of the in-text ads so the pop-up ads are different So you go to a specific URL or so for example What's happened here is somebody with the software installed on their computer went to this Miriam Webster online dictionary for the specific Word right there it looks like they're looking up a word in the dictionary and Netflix decided for whatever reason that that would be a good place for them to pop up right so their ad pops up here And it's a big graphical banner with call to action click here button so Big benefit driven clear message clear call to action Pop-up that shows up in front of the web page that they're trying to get to so This is the pop-up ads that you'll be able to deliver now that you have now that you're gonna get your PB be All set up so Those are those are the ads now the next thing we're gonna look at is the targeting So so you have an ad provider you understand the two different kinds of ads You get some ideas in your head. Hopefully on the different different things you can display in text or on banners and things like that two pieces left targeting and then lastly optimization so What you need to do and the next video I have here I'm gonna I'm gonna show you this you need to go out and you need to find the keywords that you want to target I'll teach you how to how to find some how to find the best ones and Things like that, but you need to find those keywords that you want to target and then Also the URL targets so let me explain the difference here. So when you are running a PPV campaign You can target For let's just talk about the display ads so the display ads you can target Keyword phrases so if somebody's in a search engine like Google or Yahoo or something like that and They're searching a specific phrase Improve golf swing and They and you have and you're bidding for that phrase then you're adult you'll your ad that's a chance to To be delivered right there to the user so in search bars and in any kind of Text editable field where they're typing in Specific keyword phrases that's when you're gonna get displays, okay? You're gonna get your impressions and that's how the keywords work in a Text ad the keywords Work like I mentioned before so there's a paragraph of text So they're reading a Wikipedia article about about improving their golf swing and when now when the key phrases show up They'll be highlighted and underlined and when they mouse over from your ad shows up So you want to make sure you're getting the right keyword targets there and that's how the keywords work The URL targets are only for the display ads the pop-up ads Okay, it doesn't work for the in-text ads in texture are only working with keywords the URL targets you go out and you find your competitors The ones that you know, you think you're gonna have the best traffic the best targeted audience Relevant to you. We're gonna go out and find those Competitors and you're gonna actually target their URLs you can get as general as just their root domain You know golf experts comm or you can get as specific as going specifically To an article or or page on their domain So not instead of not just popping every time somebody shows up on golf experts calm But instead having your ad pop up when they specifically go to the article about golf swings, right? On that domain. So that's how you're gonna use the URL targets and in the next video. I will show you How to go out and grab a bunch of those to start targeting now quick note with with targeting is as you increase your targets You're increasing your online presence. And so that's how easy PPV is if you want more exposure Just go out and find more targets Optimize forum and and then your ads being displayed more, right? It's that simple. So That's that and we'll give you some Visuals on that in the coming video and then when it comes to optimization, I'm gonna in the following video I'm gonna show you What you should look for in the short term what should look for in the long term And how in the long term you to look at? Your cost per target and then the short term we need to look at how you're ranking per target Now tip to remember and I'll get into this when we're When we're digging in in the back end to some of the targets that depending on what your your your Goal CPA is what your cost per acquisition is so what you're willing to spend to get a new customer Sometimes it's actually better to be in second or third place when you're bidding on these targets and not always fight for first place Because sometimes first place is expensive. I mean you'll you'll find that somebody in first place Could be spending, you know 50% more than the person in second place and while you'll get a little bit lower volume by by not being that first place Bitter on your target Your traffic is gonna come a lot cheaper So maybe that's worth it for you depending on what your goals are if you need volume you could fight to be the leader But if you just need if you can handle lower volume across larger range of targets, then you can get cheaper traffic that way so I'll go into that too So I'll see you guys on the next video as we learn how to dominate your space in the PPV world
